# UC-10.16.14 · Symantec Endpoint Protection Client Scan Threat Findings

## Description

Client scan logs that mention infections or threats reveal endpoints where on-demand or scheduled scans are still finding malware after real-time protection, often due to dormant files or policy gaps.

## Value

Prioritizes hosts needing deeper investigation when scans—not just real-time engines—report malicious content, supporting hunt and vulnerability management handoffs.

## Implementation

Ingest Client Scan log dumps into the SEP TA. Because scan log field names vary by SEPM version, validate extracted fields (`Scan_Type`, threat counters) in Search before production alerts; replace the inline `search` filter with explicit field thresholds when available. Exclude backup admin paths that routinely flag quarantine archives.

## SPL

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:scan:file" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval scan_type=coalesce(Scan_Type, scan_type, "unknown")
| search infect* OR threat* OR virus*
| stats count by host, scan_type
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (host × scan type), timechart of matching scan events, single value (distinct hosts).

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2772)
